title: ranunculus nemorosus subsp. mixtus
intro: Ranunculus nemorosus subsp. mixtus, also known as the woodland crowfoot, is a subspecies of the Ranunculus nemorosus plant, a perennial plant belonging to the buttercup family. The medicinal properties of Ranunculus nemorosus subsp. mixtus lie in its roots, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and provide relief from respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of Ranunculus nemorosus subsp. mixtus typically involve infusions of its roots, which can be used as teas or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
